Fees.Borrow Interest: All interest paid by borrowers from all markets (excluding GHO).

Revenue.Borrow Interest: A portion of interest paid by borrowers from all markets (excluding GHO).

Revenue.Liquidation Fees: A portion of fees from liquidation penalty and bonuses.
Revenue.Flashloan Fees: A portion of fees paid by flashloan borrowers and executors.

SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest: Amount of interest distributed to lenders from all markets (excluding GHO).

SupplySideRevenue.Flashloan Fees: Flashloan fees paid by flashloan borrowers and executors are distributed to lenders.

ProtocolRevenue.Borrow Interest: Amount of interest distributed to lenders from all markets (excluding GHO) are collected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Borrow Interest GHO: All interest paid on GHO market are collected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Liquidation Fees: A portion of fees from liquidation penalty and bonuses are colected by Aave treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Flashloan Fees: A portion of fees paid by flashloan borrowers and executors are collected by Aave treasury.

methodology.Fees: total fees paid by users from Colony DEX activities.
methodology.Revenue: Revenue distributed to token stakers includes 100% from CLY staking and unstaking fees, 50% of CAI fees, 8% of early-stage activity fees (ceTokens), and 70% of Validator Program activity revenues. This distribution creates a strong APY for CLY stakers, incentivizing protocol staking and long-term involvement in Colony's protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Revenue sources directly retained by the protocol include 50% from CAI fees (minting 0.20%, redemption 0.5%, and management 1%), 2% from early-stage platform activities (ceTokens distribution), 2% from USDC fundraised by a project, 16.66% from Colony DEX swap fees, and 30% from Validator Program activities. These funds support the protocol's ongoing development and operations.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 83.33% of the fees collected from Colony DEX transactions are distributed to liquidity providers. Additionally, 10% of ceTokens are distributed to staked liquidity providers on Colony's DEX, incentivizing them to continue providing liquidity and supporting the exchange. Additional revenue can be generated through farm rewards based on specific marketing campaigns and incentive programs.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Revenue distributed to token stakers includes 100% from CLY staking and unstaking fees, 50% of CAI fees, 8% of early-stage activity fees (ceTokens), and 70% of Validator Program activity revenues. This distribution creates a strong APY for CLY stakers, incentivizing protocol staking and long-term involvement in Colony's protocol.

Fees.bidnames: Action in the eosio.system contract to place a bid on premium (short <12 character) account names via auction.
Fees.ramfee: System account (eosio.ramfee) that collects the 0.5% fee from RAM buy/sell transactions to fund network operations.
Fees.cpuloan: REX-related action (e.g., fundcpuloan or defcpuloan) to manage funding or deferring CPU resource loans from staked tokens for temporary bandwidth boosts.
Fees.netloan: Database table (netloan) in the system contract tracking active REX loans specifically for NET bandwidth resources rented via actions like rentnet.
Fees.powerup: System action to pay a fee (in core tokens) for renting a fractional share of CPU and/or NET resources from the chain-owned pool for a configurable period (typically 24 hours), replacing staking/REX as the primary resource allocation model.

methodology.UserFees:
A minor fee is collected on each swap, functioning as trading fees.
The fees are set at 0.07% on Ethereum and 0.1% on other chains.
On other networks, fees may vary between different pairs and chains.
Refer to https://docs.smardex.io/overview/what-is-smardex/fees for detailed information.
methodology.Fees:
A minor fee is collected on each swap, functioning as trading fees.
The fees are set at 0.07% on Ethereum and 0.1% on other chains.
On other networks, fees may vary between different pairs and chains.
Refer to https://docs.smardex.io/overview/what-is-smardex/fees for detailed information.
methodology.Revenue: 0.02% of each swap on Ethereum is collected for staking pool (SDEX holders that staked). On other chains, fees are collected for liquidity providers and fees may vary between different pairs and chains. Refer to https://docs.smardex.io/overview/what-is-smardex/fees for detailed information.

methodology.HoldersRevenue: 0.02% of each swap on Ethereum is collected for staking pool (SDEX holders that staked). On other chains staking is not available and fees are collected for buybacks SDEX and burns.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 0.05% of each swap on Ethereum is collected for liquidity providers. On other chains, fees collected for liquidity providers and fees may vary between different pairs and chains. Refer to https://docs.smardex.io/overview/what-is-smardex/fees for detailed information.

methodology.Revenue: Protocol revenue represents the share of swap fees diverted to the protocol. This share is set on a per-pool basis and can be updated by governance. Default share is 0%.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Calculated per-pool. feeProtocol is a uint8 containing two uint4 values: feeProtocol0 (lower 4 bits) and feeProtocol1 (upper 4 bits). If feeProtocol0 = x1 and feeProtocol1 = x2, protocol revenue = Total Fees * (1/x1 + 1/x2) / 2. If only one is set, protocol revenue = Total Fees * 1/x.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Money going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: The portion of swap fees distributed to Liquidity Providers (LPs). This is (Total Fees - Protocol Revenue) for each pool.

methodology.Fees: Total fees from both Auction-based Mining and Block-based Mining. Auction: Dutch auction mechanism where price doubles after each bid then decreases to 0 over 1 hour. Block: Players deploy SOL to mine blocks and earn tokens.
methodology.Revenue: Combined protocol revenue from both mining types. AUCTION (after Dec 17, 2025): 16% total - 10% buyback/burn, 3% staking, 2% liquidity, 1% dev. BLOCK: 9% total - 8% buyback/burn, 0.5% staking, 0.5% protocol fee.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol keeps (excludes staking to holders and motherlodes). AUCTION: 13% (10% buyback/burn + 2% liquidity + 1% dev). BLOCK: 8.5% (8% buyback/burn + 0.5% protocol fee).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Returned to participants. AUCTION: 84% to sellers (previous position owners). BLOCK: 91% to players (88% direct + 3% motherlodes when explode).
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Staking rewards for $MACARON token holders. AUCTION: 3%. BLOCK: 0.5%. Combined from both mining mechanisms.

methodology.Fees: Sum of AMM trading fees (Swap.netFwFee), variable-rate borrower interest (AccrueInterest.interest), fixed-rate CT interest claims plus post-expiry treasury FW accrual (Coupon Token events), and liquidation penalties paid to external liquidators.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol share from AMM reserve fees (Swap.netFwToReserve), variable-rate borrow interest via market fee rate, and fixed-rate interest fees (CollectInterestFee) plus post-expiry treasury accrual (TreasuryFwInterestAccrued).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol share from AMM reserve fees (Swap.netFwToReserve), variable-rate borrow interest via market fee rate, and fixed-rate interest fees (CollectInterestFee) plus post-expiry treasury accrual (TreasuryFwInterestAccrued).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: AMM swap fees after reserve share, variable-rate interest to lenders, fixed-rate CT interest paid to users (RedeemInterest.interestOut), and liquidation penalties paid out to external liquidators.

Fees.Token Swap Fees: Total AMM trading fees from Swap events (netFwFee).
Fees.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: total interest from AccrueInterest. Fixed-rate: CT addresses from BondToken.CT() for BTs from AMM CreateNewMarket and fixed lending loan tokens; then RedeemInterest, CollectInterestFee, and TreasuryFwInterestAccrued in FW per CT.

Revenue.Token Swap Fees: Protocol reserve share of AMM trading fees from Swap.netFwToReserve.
Revenue.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: protocol share via market fee. Fixed-rate: CollectInterestFee and TreasuryFwInterestAccrued.

SupplySideRevenue.Token Swap Fees: AMM swap fees distributed outside protocol reserve.
SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: borrow interest to lenders. Fixed-rate: RedeemInterest to CT holders.
SupplySideRevenue.Liquidation Fees: Liquidation penalty estimated as collateral value seized minus debt repaid, converted via market oracle price.

methodology.Fees: Interest paid by borrowers across every reserve on Flying Tulip Lend, estimated as borrows * IRM.irmSampleAPR(util) * windowSeconds / year. State and rate samples read via LendingLens.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol retains no reserve factor on chain. All borrower interest is routed back to suppliers as FT denominated rewards, so protocol revenue is 0.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue, tracked as 0.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Total borrower interest. The on chain reserves accumulator collects interest first, then the protocol uses those fees to buy FT on the open market and distributes the FT to suppliers quarterly through the EpochRewardsVault. FT has a fixed total supply so nothing is minted.

Fees.Borrow Interest: Interest paid by borrowers across every reserve, estimated as borrows * IRM.irmSampleAPR(util) * windowSeconds / year.

SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest To Lenders: Total borrower interest accrues to the on chain reserves accumulator; the protocol then buys FT on the open market with these fees and distributes it to suppliers quarterly through the EpochRewardsVault.

methodology.Fees: Yield skimmed from eva vault backing after holder obligations are covered.
methodology.Revenue: eva retains no protocol revenue from skimmed yield.

SupplySideRevenue.Assets Yields: Skimmed yield allocated back to users through LP incentives.

methodology.Fees: Service fee (1%) pulled atomically user → treasury inside FrenFlow's FeeCollector contract on Polygon at Polymarket trade settlement.
methodology.UserFees: 100% of fees come directly from end-user wallets (the fee is transferFrom'd from the trader at fill).
methodology.Revenue: All service fees flow directly to the FrenFlow treasury. No liquidity providers.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue — 100% of collected fees are retained by the protocol.

Fees.Service Fees: Per-trade 1% service fee on Polymarket trades routed through FrenFlow. Denominated in USDC.e. Tracked from the `FeeCollected` event on the FeeCollector contract.

UserFees.Service Fees: Same as Fees — paid directly from the trader's wallet.

Revenue.Service Fees: 100% of collected service fees flow to the FrenFlow treasury at `0xb9e912e55454Ce284C38ccFED5b7fbbF327E689b`.

ProtocolRevenue.Service Fees: Same as Revenue — fully retained by the protocol.

methodology.Fees: Native and ZRO fees paid by users for cross-chain messages routed through the LayerZero V1 endpoint, summed from WithdrawNative and WithdrawZRO events on UltraLightNodeV2 contracts across supported chains. Withdrawals lag the actual send() events by the worker/treasury withdrawal cadence; totals converge over time.
methodology.Revenue: ZRO protocol fees moved from UltraLightNodeV2 to the LayerZero V1 Treasury contract via WithdrawZRO. Historically the V1 protocol take rate has been ~0, so this value is typically negligible. Tokens flow to the Foundation treasury and are not burnt by the contract.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: ZRO protocol fees collected by the LayerZero V1 Treasury contract.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Native fees paid out to V1 relayers and oracles via WithdrawNative. Includes a small protocol-native portion when treasury chooses native payment, but in practice the protocol take is ~0 so this is effectively all worker revenue.

Fees.Service Fees: Native fees paid out to relayers, oracles, and treasury via WithdrawNative.
Fees.Protocol Fees: ZRO protocol fees withdrawn from UltraLightNodeV2 by the V1 Treasury contract via WithdrawZRO.

Revenue.Protocol Fees: ZRO protocol fees moved to the V1 Treasury contract.

SupplySideRevenue.Service Fees: Native fees paid out to relayers and oracles.

methodology.Fees: Mint and redeem fees charged by the ftUSD MintAndRedeem engine on every mint/redeem, summed from Minted and Redeemed events.
methodology.Revenue: All ftUSD mint/redeem fees are sent to the Flying Tulip treasury via sweepFees, so revenue equals fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue. Fees accumulate in the engine and the owner sweeps them to the treasury (0x1118e1c057211306a40A4d7006C040dbfE1370Cb).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Not applicable. ftUSD does not pay yield to holders directly. Stakers earn separately via sftUSD/EpochRewardsVault, funded by FT bought on the open market with these fees.

Fees.Mint Fee: feeFtUSDAmount field of the MintAndRedeem.Minted event, summed over the day window.
Fees.Redeem Fee: feeFtUSDAmount field of the MintAndRedeem.Redeemed event, summed over the day window.

methodology.Fees: Fees extracted per round: 1% admin fee on the total ETH deployed and 10% vault fee on losers pool after admin. Variable effective rate depending on winner/loser ratio.

Fees.Vault Fees: The Vault fee (10% of losers pool after admin) is bridged to Ethereum and then used to buyback SPICE, 90% is burned and 10% is distributed to SPICE Stakers in Arbitrum.

methodology.Fees: We fetch daily transaction fees from NearBlocks API. The data is aggregated daily and includes all transaction fees paid on the NEAR blockchain. 70% of transaction fees are burned, while 30% can optionally be allocated to smart contract developers as rewards if they specify a fee percentage for their contracts (otherwise 100% is burned). Note that validators do not earn transaction fees - their rewards come from protocol-level inflation.
methodology.Revenue: All fees paid by users while using Near blockchain.

methodology.Fees: Total transaction fees paid on Noble chain
methodology.Revenue: Burned coins
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: All the transaction fees go to validators (100% of fees)

methodology.UserFees: Users pay $0 for transactions. sFUEL (gas token) is obtained free from faucets and has no market value.
methodology.Fees: SKALE uses sFUEL (SKALE Fuel), a gas token distributed FREE via faucets. While transactions consume gas on-chain, sFUEL has $0 monetary value. User fees are $0.
methodology.Revenue: Network revenue comes from SKL token staking subscriptions by dApp developers, not from transaction fees. On-chain transaction fees are $0 as sFUEL is free.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol revenue is generated through SKL staking model where dApp developers stake tokens for network access. Transaction fees contribute $0 as sFUEL is valueless.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Validators/nodes are compensated through SKL staking rewards, not transaction fees. On-chain gas fees in sFUEL are $0.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: No sFUEL is burned or distributed as revenue since it has no value. Revenue model is entirely based on SKL token staking.
